1→# Daemon Mode: launchd (macOS)
     2→
     3→> On macOS, Roko can run as a persistent background daemon managed by launchd. The
     4→> `roko daemon --install` command generates a launchd plist, installs it to
     5→> `~/Library/LaunchAgents/`, and starts the service. This document covers the plist
     6→> generation, lifecycle commands, IPC over Unix socket, log management, and the daemon's
     7→> event subscription model.
     8→
     9→
    10→> **Implementation**: Specified
    11→
    12→---
    13→
    14→## Overview
    15→
    16→Daemon mode transforms Roko from a CLI tool you run manually into a persistent background
    17→service that:
    18→
    19→- **Watches repositories** for changes (file system events, git push webhooks, cron schedules)
    20→- **Triggers plan execution** automatically when PRDs change or on schedule
    21→- **Maintains state** across reboots (launchd restarts it automatically)
    22→- **Accepts commands** via a Unix domain socket IPC interface
    23→- **Streams events** to connected clients (TUI, web dashboard, CI hooks)
    24→
    25→On macOS, this uses launchd — the native service manager. The `roko daemon` subcommand handles
    26→all lifecycle operations: install, start, stop, restart, uninstall, status, and log viewing.
    27→
    28→---
    29→
    30→## The `roko daemon` Subcommand
    31→
    32→```
    33→roko daemon [COMMAND]
    34→
    35→Commands:
    36→  install      Generate launchd plist and load it (starts on login)
    37→  uninstall    Unload and remove launchd plist
    38→  start        Start the daemon (if installed but not running)
    39→  stop         Stop the daemon
    40→  restart      Stop and start the daemon
    41→  status       Show daemon status (running, PID, uptime, subscriptions)
    42→  logs         Tail daemon logs (stdout + stderr)
    43→  send <cmd>   Send a command to the running daemon via IPC
    44→```
    45→
    46→### Installation
    47→
    48→```bash
    49→$ roko daemon install
    50→
    51→[1/4] Generating launchd plist...
    52→[2/4] Writing to ~/Library/LaunchAgents/dev.nunchi.roko.plist
    53→[3/4] Loading plist (launchctl load)...
    54→[4/4] Verifying daemon started...
    55→
    56→Roko daemon installed and running.
    57→  PID:      12345
    58→  Socket:   /tmp/roko-daemon.sock
    59→  Logs:     ~/.local/state/roko/daemon.log
    60→  Config:   ~/.config/roko/config.toml
    61→
    62→Daemon will start automatically on login.
    63→To stop: roko daemon stop
    64→To uninstall: roko daemon uninstall
    65→```
    66→
    67→---
    68→
    69→## launchd Plist
    70→
    71→The generated plist lives at `~/Library/LaunchAgents/dev.nunchi.roko.plist`. It uses the
    72→user-level LaunchAgents directory (not system-level LaunchDaemons), so no root access is
    73→required.
    74→
    75→```xml
    76→<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
    77→<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN"
    78→  "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
    79→<plist version="1.0">
    80→<dict>
    81→    <!-- Service identifier -->
    82→    <key>Label</key>
    83→    <string>dev.nunchi.roko</string>
    84→
    85→    <!-- Binary and arguments -->
    86→    <key>ProgramArguments</key>
    87→    <array>
    88→        <string>/Users/USERNAME/.cargo/bin/roko</string>
    89→        <string>daemon</string>
    90→        <string>run</string>
    91→        <string>--socket</string>
    92→        <string>/tmp/roko-daemon.sock</string>
    93→    </array>
    94→
    95→    <!-- Start on login (persistent daemon) -->
    96→    <key>RunAtLoad</key>
    97→    <true/>
    98→
    99→    <!-- Restart on crash (up to 10 times, then back off) -->
   100→    <key>KeepAlive</key>
   101→    <dict>
   102→        <key>SuccessfulExit</key>
   103→        <false/>
   104→    </dict>
   105→
   106→    <!-- Throttle restarts: minimum 10 seconds between restart attempts -->
   107→    <key>ThrottleInterval</key>
   108→    <integer>10</integer>
   109→
   110→    <!-- Log output -->
   111→    <key>StandardOutPath</key>
   112→    <string>/Users/USERNAME/.local/state/roko/daemon.stdout.log</string>
   113→    <key>StandardErrorPath</key>
   114→    <string>/Users/USERNAME/.local/state/roko/daemon.stderr.log</string>
   115→
   116→    <!-- Working directory -->
   117→    <key>WorkingDirectory</key>
   118→    <string>/Users/USERNAME</string>
   119→
   120→    <!-- Environment variables -->
   121→    <key>EnvironmentVariables</key>
   122→    <dict>
   123→        <key>RUST_LOG</key>
   124→        <string>info</string>
   125→        <key>HOME</key>
   126→        <string>/Users/USERNAME</string>
   127→        <key>PATH</key>
   128→        <string>/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/Users/USERNAME/.cargo/bin</string>
   129→    </dict>
   130→
   131→    <!-- Resource limits -->
   132→    <key>SoftResourceLimits</key>
   133→    <dict>
   134→        <key>NumberOfFiles</key>
   135→        <integer>4096</integer>
   136→    </dict>
   137→
   138→    <!-- Nice value: slightly lower priority than interactive processes -->
   139→    <key>Nice</key>
   140→    <integer>5</integer>
   141→</dict>
   142→</plist>
   143→```
   144→
   145→### Plist Generation in Rust
   146→
   147→The daemon install command generates this plist dynamically, substituting the current user's
   148→home directory, the resolved binary path, and any configuration overrides:
   149→
   150→```rust
   151→/// Generate launchd plist content for the daemon.
   152→fn generate_plist(config: &DaemonConfig) -> String {
   153→    let binary_path = std::env::current_exe()
   154→        .unwrap_or_else(|_| PathBuf::from("roko"));
   155→    let home = std::env::var("HOME")
   156→        .unwrap_or_else(|_| String::from("/tmp"));
   157→    let state_dir = format!("{}/.local/state/roko", home);
   158→
   159→    // Ensure state directory exists
   160→    std::fs::create_dir_all(&state_dir).ok();
   161→
   162→    format!(r#"<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
   163→<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN"
   164→  "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
   165→<plist version="1.0">
   166→<dict>
   167→    <key>Label</key>
   168→    <string>dev.nunchi.roko</string>
   169→    <key>ProgramArguments</key>
   170→    <array>
   171→        <string>{binary}</string>
   172→        <string>daemon</string>
   173→        <string>run</string>
   174→        <string>--socket</string>
   175→        <string>{socket}</string>
   176→    </array>
   177→    <key>RunAtLoad</key>
   178→    <true/>
   179→    <key>KeepAlive</key>
   180→    <dict>
   181→        <key>SuccessfulExit</key>
   182→        <false/>
   183→    </dict>
   184→    <key>ThrottleInterval</key>
   185→    <integer>10</integer>
   186→    <key>StandardOutPath</key>
   187→    <string>{state_dir}/daemon.stdout.log</string>
   188→    <key>StandardErrorPath</key>
   189→    <string>{state_dir}/daemon.stderr.log</string>
   190→    <key>WorkingDirectory</key>
   191→    <string>{home}</string>
   192→    <key>EnvironmentVariables</key>
   193→    <dict>
   194→        <key>RUST_LOG</key>
   195→        <string>{log_level}</string>
   196→        <key>HOME</key>
   197→        <string>{home}</string>
   198→        <key>PATH</key>
   199→        <string>/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:{home}/.cargo/bin</string>
   200→    </dict>
   201→    <key>SoftResourceLimits</key>
   202→    <dict>
   203→        <key>NumberOfFiles</key>
   204→        <integer>4096</integer>
   205→    </dict>
   206→    <key>Nice</key>
   207→    <integer>5</integer>
   208→</dict>
   209→</plist>"#,
   210→        binary = binary_path.display(),
   211→        socket = config.socket_path.display(),
   212→        state_dir = state_dir,
   213→        home = home,
   214→        log_level = config.log_level,
   215→    )
   216→}
   217→```
   218→
   219→---
   220→
   221→## Lifecycle Commands
   222→
   223→### Install and Load
   224→
   225→```rust
   226→/// Install the launchd plist and start the daemon.
   227→fn daemon_install(config: &DaemonConfig) -> Result<()> {
   228→    let plist_path = plist_path()?;
   229→
   230→    // Check if already installed
   231→    if plist_path.exists() {
   232→        eprintln!("Daemon already installed at {}", plist_path.display());
   233→        eprintln!("Use 'roko daemon restart' to restart, or 'roko daemon uninstall' first.");
   234→        return Ok(());
   235→    }
   236→
   237→    // Generate and write plist
   238→    let content = generate_plist(config);
   239→    std::fs::write(&plist_path, &content)?;
   240→
   241→    // Load the plist (starts the daemon)
   242→    let status = std::process::Command::new("launchctl")
   243→        .args(["load", "-w"])
   244→        .arg(&plist_path)
   245→        .status()?;
   246→
   247→    if !status.success() {
   248→        anyhow::bail!("launchctl load failed with status {}", status);
   249→    }
   250→
   251→    // Wait briefly and verify it started
   252→    std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_secs(2));
   253→    daemon_status(config)?;
   254→
   255→    Ok(())
   256→}
   257→
   258→fn plist_path() -> Result<PathBuf> {
   259→    let home = std::env::var("HOME")?;
   260→    Ok(PathBuf::from(home)
   261→        .join("Library/LaunchAgents/dev.nunchi.roko.plist"))
   262→}
   263→```
   264→
   265→### Uninstall
   266→
   267→```rust
   268→/// Unload and remove the launchd plist.
   269→fn daemon_uninstall() -> Result<()> {
   270→    let plist_path = plist_path()?;
   271→
   272→    if !plist_path.exists() {
   273→        eprintln!("Daemon not installed.");
   274→        return Ok(());
   275→    }
   276→
   277→    // Unload the plist (stops the daemon)
   278→    let _ = std::process::Command::new("launchctl")
   279→        .args(["unload", "-w"])
   280→        .arg(&plist_path)
   281→        .status();
   282→
   283→    // Remove the plist file
   284→    std::fs::remove_file(&plist_path)?;
   285→
   286→    eprintln!("Daemon uninstalled.");
   287→    Ok(())
   288→}
   289→```
   290→
   291→### Status
   292→
   293→```bash
   294→$ roko daemon status
   295→
   296→Roko Daemon Status
   297→  State:          running
   298→  PID:            12345
   299→  Uptime:         3d 14h 22m
   300→  Socket:         /tmp/roko-daemon.sock
   301→  Config:         ~/.config/roko/config.toml
   302→  Log (stdout):   ~/.local/state/roko/daemon.stdout.log
   303→  Log (stderr):   ~/.local/state/roko/daemon.stderr.log
   304→
   305→Subscriptions:
   306→  ~/dev/project-a    cron: */30 * * * *    last: 2h ago (success)
   307→  ~/dev/project-b    watch: .roko/prd/     last: 15m ago (running)
   308→  ~/dev/project-c    webhook: POST /hook   last: never
   309→```
   310→
   311→---
   312→
   313→## IPC: Unix Domain Socket
   314→
   315→The daemon exposes a Unix domain socket at `/tmp/roko-daemon.sock` for command-and-control from
   316→the CLI, TUI, or other local processes. The protocol is newline-delimited JSON:
   317→
   318→```rust
   319→/// Commands the daemon accepts over IPC.
   320→#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   321→#[serde(tag = "type")]
   322→enum DaemonCmd {
   323→    /// Get daemon status
   324→    Status,
   325→    /// Trigger a plan run for a specific repo
   326→    RunPlan { repo_path: PathBuf, plan_dir: Option<String> },
   327→    /// Add a repository subscription
   328→    Subscribe { repo_path: PathBuf, schedule: SubscriptionSchedule },
   329→    /// Remove a repository subscription
   330→    Unsubscribe { repo_path: PathBuf },
   331→    /// List all subscriptions
   332→    ListSubscriptions,
   333→    /// Stream events (daemon sends events until client disconnects)
   334→    StreamEvents { filter: Option<EventFilter> },
   335→    /// Graceful shutdown
   336→    Shutdown,
   337→}
   338→
   339→/// Responses from the daemon.
   340→#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   341→#[serde(tag = "type")]
   342→enum DaemonResponse {
   343→    Ok { message: String },
   344→    Error { message: String },
   345→    Status(DaemonStatus),
   346→    Subscriptions(Vec<Subscription>),
   347→    Event(DaemonEvent),
   348→}
   349→```
   350→
   351→### IPC Server Implementation
   352→
   353→```rust
   354→/// Start the IPC server on a Unix domain socket.
   355→async fn start_ipc_server(
   356→    socket_path: &Path,
   357→    state: Arc<DaemonState>,
   358→) -> Result<()> {
   359→    // Remove stale socket file
   360→    let _ = std::fs::remove_file(socket_path);
   361→
   362→    let listener = tokio::net::UnixListener::bind(socket_path)?;
   363→
   364→    loop {
   365→        let (stream, _) = listener.accept().await?;
   366→        let state = state.clone();
   367→        tokio::spawn(async move {
   368→            handle_ipc_connection(stream, state).await;
   369→        });
   370→    }
   371→}
   372→```
   373→
   374→### CLI IPC Client
   375→
   376→```bash
   377→# Send a command to the running daemon
   378→$ roko daemon send status
   379→# Connects to /tmp/roko-daemon.sock, sends {"type":"Status"}, prints response
   380→
   381→$ roko daemon send run-plan --repo ~/dev/my-project
   382→# Triggers plan execution for the specified repo
   383→
   384→$ roko daemon send subscribe --repo ~/dev/my-project --cron "*/30 * * * *"
   385→# Adds a cron-based subscription
   386→```
   387→
   388→---
   389→
   390→## Log Management
   391→
   392→Daemon logs go to XDG state directory:
   393→
   394→- **stdout**: `~/.local/state/roko/daemon.stdout.log` — structured info/debug output
   395→- **stderr**: `~/.local/state/roko/daemon.stderr.log` — errors and warnings
   396→
   397→The `roko daemon logs` command tails both:
   398→
   399→```bash
   400→$ roko daemon logs
   401→# Equivalent to: tail -f ~/.local/state/roko/daemon.stdout.log
   402→
   403→$ roko daemon logs --stderr
   404→# Equivalent to: tail -f ~/.local/state/roko/daemon.stderr.log
   405→
   406→$ roko daemon logs --lines 100
   407→# Show last 100 lines
   408→```
   409→
   410→### Log Rotation
   411→
   412→launchd does not provide built-in log rotation. The daemon implements its own:
   413→
   414→- Maximum log file size: 10MB
   415→- On reaching the limit, rename `daemon.stdout.log` to `daemon.stdout.log.1` and start fresh
   416→- Keep at most 3 rotated files (`.log.1`, `.log.2`, `.log.3`)
   417→- Total maximum disk usage for logs: ~40MB
   418→
   419→Alternatively, on macOS 13+, configure the system log via `os_log` for integration with
   420→Console.app and the unified logging system.
   421→
   422→---
   423→
   424→## Daemon Startup Sequence
   425→
   426→When the daemon starts (either via `launchctl load` or `roko daemon start`), it follows a
   427→13-step initialization:
   428→
   429→```
   430→ 1. Parse CLI args and resolve config paths
   431→ 2. Load global config (~/.config/roko/config.toml)
   432→ 3. Initialize logging (file + optional stderr)
   433→ 4. Create or connect to Unix domain socket
   434→ 5. Load subscription list from config
   435→ 6. For each subscription:
   436→    a. Validate repo path exists
   437→    b. Load repo-local config (.roko/config.toml)
   438→    c. Initialize file watcher (if watch mode)
   439→    d. Initialize cron scheduler (if cron mode)
   440→    e. Initialize webhook listener (if webhook mode)
   441→ 7. Start IPC server (accept commands on socket)
   442→ 8. Start event bus (internal pub/sub for daemon events)
   443→ 9. Start health check loop (periodic self-assessment)
   444→10. Start adaptive clock (Gamma/Theta/Delta frequencies)
   445→11. Log startup complete with PID and socket path
   446→12. Enter main event loop (process subscriptions, IPC commands, events)
   447→13. On SIGTERM/SIGINT: graceful shutdown (drain running tasks, save state, exit)
   448→```
   449→
   450→---
   451→
   452→## Graceful Shutdown
   453→
   454→When the daemon receives SIGTERM (from `launchctl unload` or `roko daemon stop`):
   455→
   456→1. **Stop accepting new tasks** — mark all subscriptions as paused
   457→2. **Drain running tasks** — wait up to 30 seconds for in-progress plan runs to complete
   458→3. **Save state** — write subscription states and any pending events to disk
   459→4. **Close IPC socket** — clean up the socket file
   460→5. **Exit cleanly** — exit code 0
   461→
   462→If tasks do not complete within the 30-second drain period, the daemon force-kills them and
   463→exits. launchd will not restart the daemon after a clean exit (only after crash exits, per the
   464→`SuccessfulExit = false` KeepAlive configuration).
   465→
   466→---
   467→
   468→## Environment Variables in launchd
   469→
   470→launchd runs daemons in a minimal environment — it does not source `~/.zshrc` or `~/.bashrc`.
   471→Environment variables that the daemon needs must be set in the plist's `EnvironmentVariables`
   472→dictionary.
   473→
   474→The `roko daemon install` command detects environment variables from the current shell and
   475→includes relevant ones in the plist:
   476→
   477→- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` — if set, included in the plist
   478→- `OPENAI_API_KEY` — if set, included in the plist
   479→- `RUST_LOG` — always included (defaults to `info`)
   480→- `HOME` — always included (launchd may not set it)
   481→- `PATH` — always included with `~/.cargo/bin` appended
   482→
   483→For sensitive keys (API keys), the daemon can alternatively read them from the macOS Keychain
   484→at runtime via the `keyring` crate, avoiding storage in the plist file. See
   485→`10-secret-management.md` for the full key resolution strategy.
   486→
   487→---
   488→
   489→## Current Status
   490→
   491→Daemon mode is at **Tier 3H** priority in the implementation plan (P2 — planned but not yet in
   492→the critical path). The `roko daemon` subcommand structure is defined in the CLI but the
   493→implementation is scaffold-only:
   494→
   495→- The `DaemonCmd` enum and IPC protocol are designed but not wired
   496→- The plist generation function is implemented but not tested end-to-end
   497→- File watching uses `notify` crate (already a workspace dependency) but is not connected to
   498→  the daemon event loop
   499→- Cron scheduling uses `cron` crate but is not integrated
   500→
   501→The prerequisite for daemon mode is completing the subscription configuration system
   502→(see `08-subscription-configuration.md`) which defines how repositories are registered and
   503→how their schedules are configured in `roko.toml`.
   504→

file.content:
# Daemon Mode: launchd (macOS)

> On macOS, Roko can run as a persistent background daemon managed by launchd. The
> `roko daemon --install` command generates a launchd plist, installs it to
> `~/Library/LaunchAgents/`, and starts the service. This document covers the plist
> generation, lifecycle commands, IPC over Unix socket, log management, and the daemon's
> event subscription model.


> **Implementation**: Specified

---

## Overview

Daemon mode transforms Roko from a CLI tool you run manually into a persistent background
service that:

- **Watches repositories** for changes (file system events, git push webhooks, cron schedules)
- **Triggers plan execution** automatically when PRDs change or on schedule
- **Maintains state** across reboots (launchd restarts it automatically)
- **Accepts commands** via a Unix domain socket IPC interface
- **Streams events** to connected clients (TUI, web dashboard, CI hooks)

On macOS, this uses launchd — the native service manager. The `roko daemon` subcommand handles
all lifecycle operations: install, start, stop, restart, uninstall, status, and log viewing.

---

## The `roko daemon` Subcommand

```
roko daemon [COMMAND]

Commands:
  install      Generate launchd plist and load it (starts on login)
  uninstall    Unload and remove launchd plist
  start        Start the daemon (if installed but not running)
  stop         Stop the daemon
  restart      Stop and start the daemon
  status       Show daemon status (running, PID, uptime, subscriptions)
  logs         Tail daemon logs (stdout + stderr)
  send <cmd>   Send a command to the running daemon via IPC
```

### Installation

```bash
$ roko daemon install

[1/4] Generating launchd plist...
[2/4] Writing to ~/Library/LaunchAgents/dev.nunchi.roko.plist
[3/4] Loading plist (launchctl load)...
[4/4] Verifying daemon started...

Roko daemon installed and running.
  PID:      12345
  Socket:   /tmp/roko-daemon.sock
  Logs:     ~/.local/state/roko/daemon.log
  Config:   ~/.config/roko/config.toml

Daemon will start automatically on login.
To stop: roko daemon stop
To uninstall: roko daemon uninstall
```

---

## launchd Plist

The generated plist lives at `~/Library/LaunchAgents/dev.nunchi.roko.plist`. It uses the
user-level LaunchAgents directory (not system-level LaunchDaemons), so no root access is
required.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN"
  "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
    <!-- Service identifier -->
    <key>Label</key>
    <string>dev.nunchi.roko</string>

    <!-- Binary and arguments -->
    <key>ProgramArguments</key>
    <array>
        <string>/Users/USERNAME/.cargo/bin/roko</string>
        <string>daemon</string>
        <string>run</string>
        <string>--socket</string>
        <string>/tmp/roko-daemon.sock</string>
    </array>

    <!-- Start on login (persistent daemon) -->
    <key>RunAtLoad</key>
    <true/>

    <!-- Restart on crash (up to 10 times, then back off) -->
    <key>KeepAlive</key>
    <dict>
        <key>SuccessfulExit</key>
        <false/>
    </dict>

    <!-- Throttle restarts: minimum 10 seconds between restart attempts -->
    <key>ThrottleInterval</key>
    <integer>10</integer>

    <!-- Log output -->
    <key>StandardOutPath</key>
    <string>/Users/USERNAME/.local/state/roko/daemon.stdout.log</string>
    <key>StandardErrorPath</key>
    <string>/Users/USERNAME/.local/state/roko/daemon.stderr.log</string>

    <!-- Working directory -->
    <key>WorkingDirectory</key>
    <string>/Users/USERNAME</string>

    <!-- Environment variables -->
    <key>EnvironmentVariables</key>
    <dict>
        <key>RUST_LOG</key>
        <string>info</string>
        <key>HOME</key>
        <string>/Users/USERNAME</string>
        <key>PATH</key>
        <string>/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/Users/USERNAME/.cargo/bin</string>
    </dict>

    <!-- Resource limits -->
    <key>SoftResourceLimits</key>
    <dict>
        <key>NumberOfFiles</key>
        <integer>4096</integer>
    </dict>

    <!-- Nice value: slightly lower priority than interactive processes -->
    <key>Nice</key>
    <integer>5</integer>
</dict>
</plist>
```

### Plist Generation in Rust

The daemon install command generates this plist dynamically, substituting the current user's
home directory, the resolved binary path, and any configuration overrides:

```rust
/// Generate launchd plist content for the daemon.
fn generate_plist(config: &DaemonConfig) -> String {
    let binary_path = std::env::current_exe()
        .unwrap_or_else(|_| PathBuf::from("roko"));
    let home = std::env::var("HOME")
        .unwrap_or_else(|_| String::from("/tmp"));
    let state_dir = format!("{}/.local/state/roko", home);

    // Ensure state directory exists
    std::fs::create_dir_all(&state_dir).ok();

    format!(r#"<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN"
  "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
    <key>Label</key>
    <string>dev.nunchi.roko</string>
    <key>ProgramArguments</key>
    <array>
        <string>{binary}</string>
        <string>daemon</string>
        <string>run</string>
        <string>--socket</string>
        <string>{socket}</string>
    </array>
    <key>RunAtLoad</key>
    <true/>
    <key>KeepAlive</key>
    <dict>
        <key>SuccessfulExit</key>
        <false/>
    </dict>
    <key>ThrottleInterval</key>
    <integer>10</integer>
    <key>StandardOutPath</key>
    <string>{state_dir}/daemon.stdout.log</string>
    <key>StandardErrorPath</key>
    <string>{state_dir}/daemon.stderr.log</string>
    <key>WorkingDirectory</key>
    <string>{home}</string>
    <key>EnvironmentVariables</key>
    <dict>
        <key>RUST_LOG</key>
        <string>{log_level}</string>
        <key>HOME</key>
        <string>{home}</string>
        <key>PATH</key>
        <string>/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:{home}/.cargo/bin</string>
    </dict>
    <key>SoftResourceLimits</key>
    <dict>
        <key>NumberOfFiles</key>
        <integer>4096</integer>
    </dict>
    <key>Nice</key>
    <integer>5</integer>
</dict>
</plist>"#,
        binary = binary_path.display(),
        socket = config.socket_path.display(),
        state_dir = state_dir,
        home = home,
        log_level = config.log_level,
    )
}
```

---

## Lifecycle Commands

### Install and Load

```rust
/// Install the launchd plist and start the daemon.
fn daemon_install(config: &DaemonConfig) -> Result<()> {
    let plist_path = plist_path()?;

    // Check if already installed
    if plist_path.exists() {
        eprintln!("Daemon already installed at {}", plist_path.display());
        eprintln!("Use 'roko daemon restart' to restart, or 'roko daemon uninstall' first.");
        return Ok(());
    }

    // Generate and write plist
    let content = generate_plist(config);
    std::fs::write(&plist_path, &content)?;

    // Load the plist (starts the daemon)
    let status = std::process::Command::new("launchctl")
        .args(["load", "-w"])
        .arg(&plist_path)
        .status()?;

    if !status.success() {
        anyhow::bail!("launchctl load failed with status {}", status);
    }

    // Wait briefly and verify it started
    std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_secs(2));
    daemon_status(config)?;

    Ok(())
}

fn plist_path() -> Result<PathBuf> {
    let home = std::env::var("HOME")?;
    Ok(PathBuf::from(home)
        .join("Library/LaunchAgents/dev.nunchi.roko.plist"))
}
```

### Uninstall

```rust
/// Unload and remove the launchd plist.
fn daemon_uninstall() -> Result<()> {
    let plist_path = plist_path()?;

    if !plist_path.exists() {
        eprintln!("Daemon not installed.");
        return Ok(());
    }

    // Unload the plist (stops the daemon)
    let _ = std::process::Command::new("launchctl")
        .args(["unload", "-w"])
        .arg(&plist_path)
        .status();

    // Remove the plist file
    std::fs::remove_file(&plist_path)?;

    eprintln!("Daemon uninstalled.");
    Ok(())
}
```

### Status

```bash
$ roko daemon status

Roko Daemon Status
  State:          running
  PID:            12345
  Uptime:         3d 14h 22m
  Socket:         /tmp/roko-daemon.sock
  Config:         ~/.config/roko/config.toml
  Log (stdout):   ~/.local/state/roko/daemon.stdout.log
  Log (stderr):   ~/.local/state/roko/daemon.stderr.log

Subscriptions:
  ~/dev/project-a    cron: */30 * * * *    last: 2h ago (success)
  ~/dev/project-b    watch: .roko/prd/     last: 15m ago (running)
  ~/dev/project-c    webhook: POST /hook   last: never
```

---

## IPC: Unix Domain Socket

The daemon exposes a Unix domain socket at `/tmp/roko-daemon.sock` for command-and-control from
the CLI, TUI, or other local processes. The protocol is newline-delimited JSON:

```rust
/// Commands the daemon accepts over IPC.
#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(tag = "type")]
enum DaemonCmd {
    /// Get daemon status
    Status,
    /// Trigger a plan run for a specific repo
    RunPlan { repo_path: PathBuf, plan_dir: Option<String> },
    /// Add a repository subscription
    Subscribe { repo_path: PathBuf, schedule: SubscriptionSchedule },
    /// Remove a repository subscription
    Unsubscribe { repo_path: PathBuf },
    /// List all subscriptions
    ListSubscriptions,
    /// Stream events (daemon sends events until client disconnects)
    StreamEvents { filter: Option<EventFilter> },
    /// Graceful shutdown
    Shutdown,
}

/// Responses from the daemon.
#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(tag = "type")]
enum DaemonResponse {
    Ok { message: String },
    Error { message: String },
    Status(DaemonStatus),
    Subscriptions(Vec<Subscription>),
    Event(DaemonEvent),
}
```

### IPC Server Implementation

```rust
/// Start the IPC server on a Unix domain socket.
async fn start_ipc_server(
    socket_path: &Path,
    state: Arc<DaemonState>,
) -> Result<()> {
    // Remove stale socket file
    let _ = std::fs::remove_file(socket_path);

    let listener = tokio::net::UnixListener::bind(socket_path)?;

    loop {
        let (stream, _) = listener.accept().await?;
        let state = state.clone();
        tokio::spawn(async move {
            handle_ipc_connection(stream, state).await;
        });
    }
}
```

### CLI IPC Client

```bash
# Send a command to the running daemon
$ roko daemon send status
# Connects to /tmp/roko-daemon.sock, sends {"type":"Status"}, prints response

$ roko daemon send run-plan --repo ~/dev/my-project
# Triggers plan execution for the specified repo

$ roko daemon send subscribe --repo ~/dev/my-project --cron "*/30 * * * *"
# Adds a cron-based subscription
```

---

## Log Management

Daemon logs go to XDG state directory:

- **stdout**: `~/.local/state/roko/daemon.stdout.log` — structured info/debug output
- **stderr**: `~/.local/state/roko/daemon.stderr.log` — errors and warnings

The `roko daemon logs` command tails both:

```bash
$ roko daemon logs
# Equivalent to: tail -f ~/.local/state/roko/daemon.stdout.log

$ roko daemon logs --stderr
# Equivalent to: tail -f ~/.local/state/roko/daemon.stderr.log

$ roko daemon logs --lines 100
# Show last 100 lines
```

### Log Rotation

launchd does not provide built-in log rotation. The daemon implements its own:

- Maximum log file size: 10MB
- On reaching the limit, rename `daemon.stdout.log` to `daemon.stdout.log.1` and start fresh
- Keep at most 3 rotated files (`.log.1`, `.log.2`, `.log.3`)
- Total maximum disk usage for logs: ~40MB

Alternatively, on macOS 13+, configure the system log via `os_log` for integration with
Console.app and the unified logging system.

---

## Daemon Startup Sequence

When the daemon starts (either via `launchctl load` or `roko daemon start`), it follows a
13-step initialization:

```
 1. Parse CLI args and resolve config paths
 2. Load global config (~/.config/roko/config.toml)
 3. Initialize logging (file + optional stderr)
 4. Create or connect to Unix domain socket
 5. Load subscription list from config
 6. For each subscription:
    a. Validate repo path exists
    b. Load repo-local config (.roko/config.toml)
    c. Initialize file watcher (if watch mode)
    d. Initialize cron scheduler (if cron mode)
    e. Initialize webhook listener (if webhook mode)
 7. Start IPC server (accept commands on socket)
 8. Start event bus (internal pub/sub for daemon events)
 9. Start health check loop (periodic self-assessment)
10. Start adaptive clock (Gamma/Theta/Delta frequencies)
11. Log startup complete with PID and socket path
12. Enter main event loop (process subscriptions, IPC commands, events)
13. On SIGTERM/SIGINT: graceful shutdown (drain running tasks, save state, exit)
```

---

## Graceful Shutdown

When the daemon receives SIGTERM (from `launchctl unload` or `roko daemon stop`):

1. **Stop accepting new tasks** — mark all subscriptions as paused
2. **Drain running tasks** — wait up to 30 seconds for in-progress plan runs to complete
3. **Save state** — write subscription states and any pending events to disk
4. **Close IPC socket** — clean up the socket file
5. **Exit cleanly** — exit code 0

If tasks do not complete within the 30-second drain period, the daemon force-kills them and
exits. launchd will not restart the daemon after a clean exit (only after crash exits, per the
`SuccessfulExit = false` KeepAlive configuration).

---

## Environment Variables in launchd

launchd runs daemons in a minimal environment — it does not source `~/.zshrc` or `~/.bashrc`.
Environment variables that the daemon needs must be set in the plist's `EnvironmentVariables`
dictionary.

The `roko daemon install` command detects environment variables from the current shell and
includes relevant ones in the plist:

- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` — if set, included in the plist
- `OPENAI_API_KEY` — if set, included in the plist
- `RUST_LOG` — always included (defaults to `info`)
- `HOME` — always included (launchd may not set it)
- `PATH` — always included with `~/.cargo/bin` appended

For sensitive keys (API keys), the daemon can alternatively read them from the macOS Keychain
at runtime via the `keyring` crate, avoiding storage in the plist file. See
`10-secret-management.md` for the full key resolution strategy.

---

## Current Status

Daemon mode is at **Tier 3H** priority in the implementation plan (P2 — planned but not yet in
the critical path). The `roko daemon` subcommand structure is defined in the CLI but the
implementation is scaffold-only:

- The `DaemonCmd` enum and IPC protocol are designed but not wired
- The plist generation function is implemented but not tested end-to-end
- File watching uses `notify` crate (already a workspace dependency) but is not connected to
  the daemon event loop
- Cron scheduling uses `cron` crate but is not integrated

The prerequisite for daemon mode is completing the subscription configuration system
(see `08-subscription-configuration.md`) which defines how repositories are registered and
how their schedules are configured in `roko.toml`.

content.content:
1→# Daemon Mode: systemd (Linux)
     2→
     3→> On Linux, Roko can run as a persistent background daemon managed by systemd. The
     4→> `roko daemon --install` command generates a systemd user unit file, installs it to
     5→> `~/.config/systemd/user/`, and enables the service. This document covers the unit file
     6→> generation, restart policies, journal integration, socket activation, and the differences
     7→> from the macOS launchd path.
     8→
     9→
    10→> **Implementation**: Specified
    11→
    12→---
    13→
    14→## Overview
    15→
    16→The Linux daemon mode mirrors the macOS launchd path (see `04-daemon-launchd-macos.md`) with
    17→identical functionality: repository watching, automatic plan execution, IPC over Unix socket,
    18→and event streaming. The difference is the service manager integration — systemd instead of
    19→launchd.
    20→
    21→The `roko daemon` subcommand detects the platform at runtime and generates the appropriate
    22→service definition:
    23→
    24→```rust
    25→fn daemon_install(config: &DaemonConfig) -> Result<()> {
    26→    #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
    27→    return install_launchd(config);
    28→
    29→    #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
    30→    return install_systemd(config);
    31→
    32→    #[cfg(not(any(target_os = "macos", target_os = "linux")))]
    33→    anyhow::bail!("Daemon mode is only supported on macOS (launchd) and Linux (systemd)");
    34→}
    35→```
    36→
    37→---
    38→
    39→## The `roko daemon` Subcommand (Linux)
    40→
    41→The same CLI interface as macOS, backed by `systemctl --user`:
    42→
    43→```
    44→roko daemon [COMMAND]
    45→
    46→Commands:
    47→  install      Generate systemd unit and enable it
    48→  uninstall    Disable and remove systemd unit
    49→  start        Start the daemon (systemctl --user start roko)
    50→  stop         Stop the daemon (systemctl --user stop roko)
    51→  restart      Restart the daemon (systemctl --user restart roko)
    52→  status       Show daemon status (systemctl --user status roko)
    53→  logs         Tail daemon logs (journalctl --user-unit roko)
    54→  send <cmd>   Send a command to the running daemon via IPC
    55→```
    56→
    57→### Installation
    58→
    59→```bash
    60→$ roko daemon install
    61→
    62→[1/4] Generating systemd unit file...
    63→[2/4] Writing to ~/.config/systemd/user/roko.service
    64→[3/4] Enabling service (systemctl --user enable roko)...
    65→[4/4] Starting service (systemctl --user start roko)...
    66→
    67→Roko daemon installed and running.
    68→  PID:      12345
    69→  Socket:   /run/user/1000/roko-daemon.sock
    70→  Logs:     journalctl --user-unit roko -f
    71→  Config:   ~/.config/roko/config.toml
    72→
    73→Daemon will start automatically on login.
    74→To stop: roko daemon stop
    75→To uninstall: roko daemon uninstall
    76→```
    77→
    78→---
    79→
    80→## systemd Unit File
    81→
    82→The generated unit file lives at `~/.config/systemd/user/roko.service`. It uses the user-level
    83→systemd instance (not system-level), so no root access is required.
    84→
    85→```ini
    86→# ~/.config/systemd/user/roko.service
    87→#
    88→# Roko cognitive agent daemon.
    89→# Generated by: roko daemon install
    90→# To modify: edit this file, then run: systemctl --user daemon-reload
    91→
    92→[Unit]
    93→Description=Roko cognitive agent daemon
    94→Documentation=https://github.com/nunchi/roko
    95→After=network-online.target
    96→Wants=network-online.target
    97→
    98→[Service]
    99→Type=simple
   100→ExecStart=/home/USERNAME/.cargo/bin/roko daemon run \
   101→    --socket %t/roko-daemon.sock
   102→
   103→# Restart policy: restart on failure, with exponential backoff
   104→Restart=on-failure
   105→RestartSec=10
   106→RestartMaxDelaySec=300
   107→RestartSteps=5
   108→
   109→# Environment
   110→Environment=RUST_LOG=info
   111→Environment=HOME=/home/USERNAME
   112→EnvironmentFile=-%h/.config/roko/daemon.env
   113→
   114→# Resource limits
   115→LimitNOFILE=4096
   116→
   117→# Security hardening
   118→NoNewPrivileges=true
   119→ProtectSystem=strict
   120→ProtectHome=read-only
   121→ReadWritePaths=%h/.roko %h/.local/state/roko %h/.config/roko
   122→PrivateTmp=true
   123→
   124→# Working directory
   125→WorkingDirectory=%h
   126→
   127→# Watchdog: systemd kills the process if it doesn't ping within 60s
   128→WatchdogSec=60
   129→
   130→[Install]
   131→WantedBy=default.target
   132→```
   133→
   134→### Key Configuration Choices
   135→
   136→**`Type=simple`**: The daemon process itself is the main process. systemd considers the service
   137→started as soon as the `ExecStart` process is running. This is correct because the daemon
   138→initializes quickly (< 1 second) and starts accepting IPC commands immediately.
   139→
   140→**`Restart=on-failure`**: systemd restarts the daemon if it exits with a non-zero exit code.
   141→Clean shutdowns (exit 0) do not trigger restart. This matches the launchd `SuccessfulExit =
   142→false` KeepAlive behavior.
   143→
   144→**`RestartSec=10` with `RestartMaxDelaySec=300` and `RestartSteps=5`**: Exponential backoff
   145→for restarts. First restart after 10 seconds, then progressively longer delays up to 5 minutes.
   146→This prevents restart storms if the daemon has a persistent failure (e.g., missing config file,
   147→permission error).
   148→
   149→**`%t/roko-daemon.sock`**: The `%t` specifier expands to `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` (typically
   150→`/run/user/1000` on Linux). This directory is per-user, tmpfs-backed, and cleaned up on logout
   151→— ideal for Unix domain sockets.
   152→
   153→**`EnvironmentFile=-%h/.config/roko/daemon.env`**: The `-` prefix means systemd ignores the
   154→file if it does not exist. This file can contain API keys and other environment variables that
   155→should not be in the unit file:
   156→
   157→```bash
   158→# ~/.config/roko/daemon.env
   159→ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
   160→OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
   161→```
   162→
   163→**Security hardening**:
   164→- `NoNewPrivileges=true` — prevents the daemon from gaining new privileges
   165→- `ProtectSystem=strict` — mounts the entire filesystem read-only except explicitly allowed paths
   166→- `ProtectHome=read-only` — mounts the home directory read-only
   167→- `ReadWritePaths=...` — allows write access only to Roko's state directories
   168→- `PrivateTmp=true` — gives the daemon its own `/tmp` namespace
   169→
   170→---
   171→
   172→## Unit File Generation in Rust
   173→
   174→```rust
   175→/// Generate systemd unit file content for the daemon.
   176→fn generate_systemd_unit(config: &DaemonConfig) -> String {
   177→    let binary_path = std::env::current_exe()
   178→        .unwrap_or_else(|_| PathBuf::from("roko"));
   179→    let home = std::env::var("HOME")
   180→        .unwrap_or_else(|_| String::from("/tmp"));
   181→
   182→    format!(r#"[Unit]
   183→Description=Roko cognitive agent daemon
   184→Documentation=https://github.com/nunchi/roko
   185→After=network-online.target
   186→Wants=network-online.target
   187→
   188→[Service]
   189→Type=simple
   190→ExecStart={binary} daemon run \
   191→    --socket %t/roko-daemon.sock
   192→
   193→Restart=on-failure
   194→RestartSec=10
   195→RestartMaxDelaySec=300
   196→RestartSteps=5
   197→
   198→Environment=RUST_LOG={log_level}
   199→Environment=HOME={home}
   200→EnvironmentFile=-{home}/.config/roko/daemon.env
   201→
   202→LimitNOFILE=4096
   203→
   204→NoNewPrivileges=true
   205→ProtectSystem=strict
   206→ProtectHome=read-only
   207→ReadWritePaths={home}/.roko {home}/.local/state/roko {home}/.config/roko
   208→PrivateTmp=true
   209→
   210→WorkingDirectory={home}
   211→
   212→WatchdogSec=60
   213→
   214→[Install]
   215→WantedBy=default.target
   216→"#,
   217→        binary = binary_path.display(),
   218→        home = home,
   219→        log_level = config.log_level,
   220→    )
   221→}
   222→```
   223→
   224→---
   225→
   226→## Lifecycle Commands (systemd Implementation)
   227→
   228→### Install and Enable
   229→
   230→```rust
   231→fn install_systemd(config: &DaemonConfig) -> Result<()> {
   232→    let unit_dir = dirs::config_dir()
   233→        .unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("~/.config"))
   234→        .join("systemd/user");
   235→    std::fs::create_dir_all(&unit_dir)?;
   236→
   237→    let unit_path = unit_dir.join("roko.service");
   238→
   239→    if unit_path.exists() {
   240→        eprintln!("Daemon already installed at {}", unit_path.display());
   241→        eprintln!("Use 'roko daemon restart' or 'roko daemon uninstall' first.");
   242→        return Ok(());
   243→    }
   244→
   245→    // Write the unit file
   246→    let content = generate_systemd_unit(config);
   247→    std::fs::write(&unit_path, &content)?;
   248→
   249→    // Reload systemd to pick up the new unit
   250→    run_systemctl(&["daemon-reload"])?;
   251→
   252→    // Enable the service (start on login)
   253→    run_systemctl(&["enable", "roko.service"])?;
   254→
   255→    // Start the service now
   256→    run_systemctl(&["start", "roko.service"])?;
   257→
   258→    // Verify it started
   259→    std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_secs(2));
   260→    daemon_status_systemd()?;
   261→
   262→    Ok(())
   263→}
   264→
   265→fn run_systemctl(args: &[&str]) -> Result<()> {
   266→    let status = std::process::Command::new("systemctl")
   267→        .arg("--user")
   268→        .args(args)
   269→        .status()?;
   270→
   271→    if !status.success() {
   272→        anyhow::bail!("systemctl --user {} failed", args.join(" "));
   273→    }
   274→    Ok(())
   275→}
   276→```
   277→
   278→### Uninstall
   279→
   280→```rust
   281→fn uninstall_systemd() -> Result<()> {
   282→    // Stop the service
   283→    let _ = run_systemctl(&["stop", "roko.service"]);
   284→
   285→    // Disable (remove from login startup)
   286→    let _ = run_systemctl(&["disable", "roko.service"]);
   287→
   288→    // Remove the unit file
   289→    let unit_path = dirs::config_dir()
   290→        .unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("~/.config"))
   291→        .join("systemd/user/roko.service");
   292→
   293→    if unit_path.exists() {
   294→        std::fs::remove_file(&unit_path)?;
   295→    }
   296→
   297→    // Reload systemd
   298→    run_systemctl(&["daemon-reload"])?;
   299→
   300→    eprintln!("Daemon uninstalled.");
   301→    Ok(())
   302→}
   303→```
   304→
   305→### Status
   306→
   307→```bash
   308→$ roko daemon status
   309→
   310→# Runs: systemctl --user status roko.service
   311→# Plus: connects to IPC socket for subscription info
   312→
   313→Roko Daemon Status
   314→  State:          active (running) since Mon 2026-04-10 09:15:00 EDT; 2 days ago
   315→  PID:            12345
   316→  Memory:         45.2M
   317→  CPU:            0.3%
   318→  Socket:         /run/user/1000/roko-daemon.sock
   319→  Config:         ~/.config/roko/config.toml
   320→
   321→Subscriptions:
   322→  ~/dev/project-a    cron: */30 * * * *    last: 2h ago (success)
   323→  ~/dev/project-b    watch: .roko/prd/     last: 15m ago (running)
   324→```
   325→
   326→---
   327→
   328→## Log Management via journald
   329→
   330→systemd's journald captures all stdout/stderr from the daemon. No separate log files needed —
   331→the journal handles rotation, compression, and retention automatically.
   332→
   333→```bash
   334→# Tail daemon logs in real time
   335→$ roko daemon logs
   336→# Equivalent to: journalctl --user-unit roko.service -f
   337→
   338→# Show last 100 lines
   339→$ roko daemon logs --lines 100
   340→# Equivalent to: journalctl --user-unit roko.service -n 100
   341→
   342→# Show logs since last boot
   343→$ roko daemon logs --boot
   344→# Equivalent to: journalctl --user-unit roko.service -b
   345→
   346→# Show logs from a specific time range
   347→$ journalctl --user-unit roko.service --since "2026-04-10" --until "2026-04-11"
   348→
   349→# JSON output for machine parsing
   350→$ journalctl --user-unit roko.service -o json
   351→```
   352→
   353→The `roko daemon logs` command wraps `journalctl` for convenience, but users can always use
   354→journalctl directly for advanced filtering.
   355→
   356→### Structured Logging
   357→
   358→The daemon uses `tracing` with the `tracing-journald` subscriber to emit structured log entries
   359→that journald stores with metadata fields:
   360→
   361→```rust
   362→use tracing::{info, warn, error};
   363→
   364→info!(
   365→    repo = %repo_path.display(),
   366→    plan = %plan_name,
   367→    "Plan execution started"
   368→);
   369→
   370→warn!(
   371→    subscription = %sub_id,
   372→    error = %err,
   373→    "Subscription check failed, will retry"
   374→);
   375→```
   376→
   377→These structured fields are queryable via journalctl:
   378→
   379→```bash
   380→# Find all log entries for a specific repo
   381→journalctl --user-unit roko.service REPO=/home/user/dev/project-a
   382→```
   383→
   384→---
   385→
   386→## Watchdog Integration
   387→
   388→The unit file includes `WatchdogSec=60`, which means the daemon must notify systemd at least
   389→once every 60 seconds that it is still healthy. If it fails to do so, systemd considers it
   390→hung and restarts it.
   391→
   392→The daemon pings the watchdog from its main event loop:
   393→
   394→```rust
   395→use sd_notify::NotifyState;
   396→
   397→// In the daemon's main event loop (runs every tick)
   398→async fn daemon_tick(state: &DaemonState) {
   399→    // Process subscriptions, IPC commands, events...
   400→
   401→    // Ping the systemd watchdog
   402→    let _ = sd_notify::notify(false, &[NotifyState::Watchdog]);
   403→}
   404→
   405→// On startup, notify systemd that the daemon is ready
   406→fn notify_ready() {
   407→    let _ = sd_notify::notify(false, &[NotifyState::Ready]);
   408→}
   409→```
   410→
   411→This uses the `sd-notify` crate (lightweight, no C dependency) to communicate with systemd's
   412→notification protocol. On macOS (where systemd is not available), the watchdog calls are no-ops.
   413→
   414→---
   415→
   416→## Socket Path Differences: macOS vs Linux
   417→
   418→| Platform | Socket Path | Why |
   419→|---|---|---|
   420→| macOS | `/tmp/roko-daemon.sock` | macOS has no per-user runtime directory by default |
   421→| Linux | `/run/user/$UID/roko-daemon.sock` | `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` is tmpfs, per-user, auto-cleaned |
   422→
   423→The `%t` specifier in the systemd unit file expands to `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR`. On Linux, this is
   424→typically `/run/user/1000` for UID 1000.
   425→
   426→The CLI automatically detects the correct socket path:
   427→
   428→```rust
   429→fn default_socket_path() -> PathBuf {
   430→    #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
   431→    {
   432→        if let Ok(runtime_dir) = std::env::var("XDG_RUNTIME_DIR") {
   433→            return PathBuf::from(runtime_dir).join("roko-daemon.sock");
   434→        }
   435→    }
   436→
   437→    // Fallback for macOS and other platforms
   438→    PathBuf::from("/tmp/roko-daemon.sock")
   439→}
   440→```
   441→
   442→---
   443→
   444→## Lingering: Daemon Without Login Session
   445→
   446→By default, systemd user services only run while the user has an active login session. For
   447→headless servers or CI machines where the daemon should run persistently (even when no user
   448→is logged in), enable lingering:
   449→
   450→```bash
   451→# Enable lingering for the current user (one-time, requires root or polkit)
   452→loginctl enable-linger $USER
   453→```
   454→
   455→With lingering enabled, the user's systemd instance starts at boot and persists after logout.
   456→The `roko daemon install` command checks for this and prints a note if lingering is not enabled:
   457→
   458→```
   459→Note: systemd user services only run while you're logged in.
   460→For persistent daemon operation on a server, run:
   461→  sudo loginctl enable-linger $USER
   462→```
   463→
   464→---
   465→
   466→## Comparison: launchd vs systemd
   467→
   468→| Feature | launchd (macOS) | systemd (Linux) |
   469→|---|---|---|
   470→| Unit file location | `~/Library/LaunchAgents/` | `~/.config/systemd/user/` |
   471→| Start on login | `RunAtLoad = true` | `WantedBy=default.target` + `enable` |
   472→| Restart on crash | `KeepAlive.SuccessfulExit = false` | `Restart=on-failure` |
   473→| Restart backoff | `ThrottleInterval` (flat) | `RestartSec` + `RestartMaxDelaySec` (exponential) |
   474→| Logs | Custom log files | journald (built-in rotation and querying) |
   475→| Socket path | `/tmp/roko-daemon.sock` | `/run/user/$UID/roko-daemon.sock` |
   476→| Security hardening | Sandbox profiles (limited) | `ProtectSystem`, `NoNewPrivileges`, namespaces |
   477→| Watchdog | Not built-in | `WatchdogSec` with `sd_notify` |
   478→| Persistent without login | Always (launchd runs at boot for user agents) | Requires `loginctl enable-linger` |
   479→| Environment | `EnvironmentVariables` dict in plist | `Environment=` directives + `EnvironmentFile=` |
   480→
   481→The daemon implementation provides identical functionality on both platforms. The platform
   482→differences are abstracted behind the `roko daemon` CLI — users do not need to know whether
   483→they are on macOS or Linux.
   484→
   485→---
   486→
   487→## Current Status
   488→
   489→The systemd integration is at the same **Tier 3H** priority as launchd daemon mode. The
   490→unit file generation is designed but not yet implemented. The IPC protocol, subscription
   491→system, and event streaming are shared between both platforms — see
   492→`04-daemon-launchd-macos.md` for the full IPC specification and
   493→`08-subscription-configuration.md` for the subscription configuration format.
   494→

file.content:
# Daemon Mode: systemd (Linux)

> On Linux, Roko can run as a persistent background daemon managed by systemd. The
> `roko daemon --install` command generates a systemd user unit file, installs it to
> `~/.config/systemd/user/`, and enables the service. This document covers the unit file
> generation, restart policies, journal integration, socket activation, and the differences
> from the macOS launchd path.


> **Implementation**: Specified

---

## Overview

The Linux daemon mode mirrors the macOS launchd path (see `04-daemon-launchd-macos.md`) with
identical functionality: repository watching, automatic plan execution, IPC over Unix socket,
and event streaming. The difference is the service manager integration — systemd instead of
launchd.

The `roko daemon` subcommand detects the platform at runtime and generates the appropriate
service definition:

```rust
fn daemon_install(config: &DaemonConfig) -> Result<()> {
    #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
    return install_launchd(config);

    #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
    return install_systemd(config);

    #[cfg(not(any(target_os = "macos", target_os = "linux")))]
    anyhow::bail!("Daemon mode is only supported on macOS (launchd) and Linux (systemd)");
}
```

---

## The `roko daemon` Subcommand (Linux)

The same CLI interface as macOS, backed by `systemctl --user`:

```
roko daemon [COMMAND]

Commands:
  install      Generate systemd unit and enable it
  uninstall    Disable and remove systemd unit
  start        Start the daemon (systemctl --user start roko)
  stop         Stop the daemon (systemctl --user stop roko)
  restart      Restart the daemon (systemctl --user restart roko)
  status       Show daemon status (systemctl --user status roko)
  logs         Tail daemon logs (journalctl --user-unit roko)
  send <cmd>   Send a command to the running daemon via IPC
```

### Installation

```bash
$ roko daemon install

[1/4] Generating systemd unit file...
[2/4] Writing to ~/.config/systemd/user/roko.service
[3/4] Enabling service (systemctl --user enable roko)...
[4/4] Starting service (systemctl --user start roko)...

Roko daemon installed and running.
  PID:      12345
  Socket:   /run/user/1000/roko-daemon.sock
  Logs:     journalctl --user-unit roko -f
  Config:   ~/.config/roko/config.toml

Daemon will start automatically on login.
To stop: roko daemon stop
To uninstall: roko daemon uninstall
```

---

## systemd Unit File

The generated unit file lives at `~/.config/systemd/user/roko.service`. It uses the user-level
systemd instance (not system-level), so no root access is required.

```ini
# ~/.config/systemd/user/roko.service
#
# Roko cognitive agent daemon.
# Generated by: roko daemon install
# To modify: edit this file, then run: systemctl --user daemon-reload

[Unit]
Description=Roko cognitive agent daemon
Documentation=https://github.com/nunchi/roko
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=/home/USERNAME/.cargo/bin/roko daemon run \
    --socket %t/roko-daemon.sock

# Restart policy: restart on failure, with exponential backoff
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=10
RestartMaxDelaySec=300
RestartSteps=5

# Environment
Environment=RUST_LOG=info
Environment=HOME=/home/USERNAME
EnvironmentFile=-%h/.config/roko/daemon.env

# Resource limits
LimitNOFILE=4096

# Security hardening
NoNewPrivileges=true
ProtectSystem=strict
ProtectHome=read-only
ReadWritePaths=%h/.roko %h/.local/state/roko %h/.config/roko
PrivateTmp=true

# Working directory
WorkingDirectory=%h

# Watchdog: systemd kills the process if it doesn't ping within 60s
WatchdogSec=60

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

### Key Configuration Choices

**`Type=simple`**: The daemon process itself is the main process. systemd considers the service
started as soon as the `ExecStart` process is running. This is correct because the daemon
initializes quickly (< 1 second) and starts accepting IPC commands immediately.

**`Restart=on-failure`**: systemd restarts the daemon if it exits with a non-zero exit code.
Clean shutdowns (exit 0) do not trigger restart. This matches the launchd `SuccessfulExit =
false` KeepAlive behavior.

**`RestartSec=10` with `RestartMaxDelaySec=300` and `RestartSteps=5`**: Exponential backoff
for restarts. First restart after 10 seconds, then progressively longer delays up to 5 minutes.
This prevents restart storms if the daemon has a persistent failure (e.g., missing config file,
permission error).

**`%t/roko-daemon.sock`**: The `%t` specifier expands to `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` (typically
`/run/user/1000` on Linux). This directory is per-user, tmpfs-backed, and cleaned up on logout
— ideal for Unix domain sockets.

**`EnvironmentFile=-%h/.config/roko/daemon.env`**: The `-` prefix means systemd ignores the
file if it does not exist. This file can contain API keys and other environment variables that
should not be in the unit file:

```bash
# ~/.config/roko/daemon.env
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
```

**Security hardening**:
- `NoNewPrivileges=true` — prevents the daemon from gaining new privileges
- `ProtectSystem=strict` — mounts the entire filesystem read-only except explicitly allowed paths
- `ProtectHome=read-only` — mounts the home directory read-only
- `ReadWritePaths=...` — allows write access only to Roko's state directories
- `PrivateTmp=true` — gives the daemon its own `/tmp` namespace

---

## Unit File Generation in Rust

```rust
/// Generate systemd unit file content for the daemon.
fn generate_systemd_unit(config: &DaemonConfig) -> String {
    let binary_path = std::env::current_exe()
        .unwrap_or_else(|_| PathBuf::from("roko"));
    let home = std::env::var("HOME")
        .unwrap_or_else(|_| String::from("/tmp"));

    format!(r#"[Unit]
Description=Roko cognitive agent daemon
Documentation=https://github.com/nunchi/roko
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart={binary} daemon run \
    --socket %t/roko-daemon.sock

Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=10
RestartMaxDelaySec=300
RestartSteps=5

Environment=RUST_LOG={log_level}
Environment=HOME={home}
EnvironmentFile=-{home}/.config/roko/daemon.env

LimitNOFILE=4096

NoNewPrivileges=true
ProtectSystem=strict
ProtectHome=read-only
ReadWritePaths={home}/.roko {home}/.local/state/roko {home}/.config/roko
PrivateTmp=true

WorkingDirectory={home}

WatchdogSec=60

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
"#,
        binary = binary_path.display(),
        home = home,
        log_level = config.log_level,
    )
}
```

---

## Lifecycle Commands (systemd Implementation)

### Install and Enable

```rust
fn install_systemd(config: &DaemonConfig) -> Result<()> {
    let unit_dir = dirs::config_dir()
        .unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("~/.config"))
        .join("systemd/user");
    std::fs::create_dir_all(&unit_dir)?;

    let unit_path = unit_dir.join("roko.service");

    if unit_path.exists() {
        eprintln!("Daemon already installed at {}", unit_path.display());
        eprintln!("Use 'roko daemon restart' or 'roko daemon uninstall' first.");
        return Ok(());
    }

    // Write the unit file
    let content = generate_systemd_unit(config);
    std::fs::write(&unit_path, &content)?;

    // Reload systemd to pick up the new unit
    run_systemctl(&["daemon-reload"])?;

    // Enable the service (start on login)
    run_systemctl(&["enable", "roko.service"])?;

    // Start the service now
    run_systemctl(&["start", "roko.service"])?;

    // Verify it started
    std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_secs(2));
    daemon_status_systemd()?;

    Ok(())
}

fn run_systemctl(args: &[&str]) -> Result<()> {
    let status = std::process::Command::new("systemctl")
        .arg("--user")
        .args(args)
        .status()?;

    if !status.success() {
        anyhow::bail!("systemctl --user {} failed", args.join(" "));
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

### Uninstall

```rust
fn uninstall_systemd() -> Result<()> {
    // Stop the service
    let _ = run_systemctl(&["stop", "roko.service"]);

    // Disable (remove from login startup)
    let _ = run_systemctl(&["disable", "roko.service"]);

    // Remove the unit file
    let unit_path = dirs::config_dir()
        .unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("~/.config"))
        .join("systemd/user/roko.service");

    if unit_path.exists() {
        std::fs::remove_file(&unit_path)?;
    }

    // Reload systemd
    run_systemctl(&["daemon-reload"])?;

    eprintln!("Daemon uninstalled.");
    Ok(())
}
```

### Status

```bash
$ roko daemon status

# Runs: systemctl --user status roko.service
# Plus: connects to IPC socket for subscription info

Roko Daemon Status
  State:          active (running) since Mon 2026-04-10 09:15:00 EDT; 2 days ago
  PID:            12345
  Memory:         45.2M
  CPU:            0.3%
  Socket:         /run/user/1000/roko-daemon.sock
  Config:         ~/.config/roko/config.toml

Subscriptions:
  ~/dev/project-a    cron: */30 * * * *    last: 2h ago (success)
  ~/dev/project-b    watch: .roko/prd/     last: 15m ago (running)
```

---

## Log Management via journald

systemd's journald captures all stdout/stderr from the daemon. No separate log files needed —
the journal handles rotation, compression, and retention automatically.

```bash
# Tail daemon logs in real time
$ roko daemon logs
# Equivalent to: journalctl --user-unit roko.service -f

# Show last 100 lines
$ roko daemon logs --lines 100
# Equivalent to: journalctl --user-unit roko.service -n 100

# Show logs since last boot
$ roko daemon logs --boot
# Equivalent to: journalctl --user-unit roko.service -b

# Show logs from a specific time range
$ journalctl --user-unit roko.service --since "2026-04-10" --until "2026-04-11"

# JSON output for machine parsing
$ journalctl --user-unit roko.service -o json
```

The `roko daemon logs` command wraps `journalctl` for convenience, but users can always use
journalctl directly for advanced filtering.

### Structured Logging

The daemon uses `tracing` with the `tracing-journald` subscriber to emit structured log entries
that journald stores with metadata fields:

```rust
use tracing::{info, warn, error};

info!(
    repo = %repo_path.display(),
    plan = %plan_name,
    "Plan execution started"
);

warn!(
    subscription = %sub_id,
    error = %err,
    "Subscription check failed, will retry"
);
```

These structured fields are queryable via journalctl:

```bash
# Find all log entries for a specific repo
journalctl --user-unit roko.service REPO=/home/user/dev/project-a
```

---

## Watchdog Integration

The unit file includes `WatchdogSec=60`, which means the daemon must notify systemd at least
once every 60 seconds that it is still healthy. If it fails to do so, systemd considers it
hung and restarts it.

The daemon pings the watchdog from its main event loop:

```rust
use sd_notify::NotifyState;

// In the daemon's main event loop (runs every tick)
async fn daemon_tick(state: &DaemonState) {
    // Process subscriptions, IPC commands, events...

    // Ping the systemd watchdog
    let _ = sd_notify::notify(false, &[NotifyState::Watchdog]);
}

// On startup, notify systemd that the daemon is ready
fn notify_ready() {
    let _ = sd_notify::notify(false, &[NotifyState::Ready]);
}
```

This uses the `sd-notify` crate (lightweight, no C dependency) to communicate with systemd's
notification protocol. On macOS (where systemd is not available), the watchdog calls are no-ops.

---

## Socket Path Differences: macOS vs Linux

| Platform | Socket Path | Why |
|---|---|---|
| macOS | `/tmp/roko-daemon.sock` | macOS has no per-user runtime directory by default |
| Linux | `/run/user/$UID/roko-daemon.sock` | `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` is tmpfs, per-user, auto-cleaned |

The `%t` specifier in the systemd unit file expands to `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR`. On Linux, this is
typically `/run/user/1000` for UID 1000.

The CLI automatically detects the correct socket path:

```rust
fn default_socket_path() -> PathBuf {
    #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
    {
        if let Ok(runtime_dir) = std::env::var("XDG_RUNTIME_DIR") {
            return PathBuf::from(runtime_dir).join("roko-daemon.sock");
        }
    }

    // Fallback for macOS and other platforms
    PathBuf::from("/tmp/roko-daemon.sock")
}
```

---

## Lingering: Daemon Without Login Session

By default, systemd user services only run while the user has an active login session. For
headless servers or CI machines where the daemon should run persistently (even when no user
is logged in), enable lingering:

```bash
# Enable lingering for the current user (one-time, requires root or polkit)
loginctl enable-linger $USER
```

With lingering enabled, the user's systemd instance starts at boot and persists after logout.
The `roko daemon install` command checks for this and prints a note if lingering is not enabled:

```
Note: systemd user services only run while you're logged in.
For persistent daemon operation on a server, run:
  sudo loginctl enable-linger $USER
```

---

## Comparison: launchd vs systemd

| Feature | launchd (macOS) | systemd (Linux) |
|---|---|---|
| Unit file location | `~/Library/LaunchAgents/` | `~/.config/systemd/user/` |
| Start on login | `RunAtLoad = true` | `WantedBy=default.target` + `enable` |
| Restart on crash | `KeepAlive.SuccessfulExit = false` | `Restart=on-failure` |
| Restart backoff | `ThrottleInterval` (flat) | `RestartSec` + `RestartMaxDelaySec` (exponential) |
| Logs | Custom log files | journald (built-in rotation and querying) |
| Socket path | `/tmp/roko-daemon.sock` | `/run/user/$UID/roko-daemon.sock` |
| Security hardening | Sandbox profiles (limited) | `ProtectSystem`, `NoNewPrivileges`, namespaces |
| Watchdog | Not built-in | `WatchdogSec` with `sd_notify` |
| Persistent without login | Always (launchd runs at boot for user agents) | Requires `loginctl enable-linger` |
| Environment | `EnvironmentVariables` dict in plist | `Environment=` directives + `EnvironmentFile=` |

The daemon implementation provides identical functionality on both platforms. The platform
differences are abstracted behind the `roko daemon` CLI — users do not need to know whether
they are on macOS or Linux.

---

## Current Status

The systemd integration is at the same **Tier 3H** priority as launchd daemon mode. The
unit file generation is designed but not yet implemented. The IPC protocol, subscription
system, and event streaming are shared between both platforms — see
`04-daemon-launchd-macos.md` for the full IPC specification and
`08-subscription-configuration.md` for the subscription configuration format.
